title: CA LCFS Quarterly Trend Sheet - Q1 2025: First Quarter of the New Year Sees a Slowdown in LCFS Deficit Generation and Credits From Biofuels
content:
Key Highlights:

In Q1 2025, LCFS credit generation declined 15.70% quarter-on-quarter, led by sharp drops in biofuels such as Bio-CNG (down 26.67%), Renewable Diesel (down 22.3%), and Biodiesel (down 27.6%). In contrast, EV charging credits rose 4.23%, driven by strong growth in onroad residential and non-residential grid EV charging for LDV/MDVs, with the latter up 166% QoQ. Deficits eased slightly overall, falling 3.13%, with diesel-related deficits dropping more steeply. The carbon intensity of electricity improved by 6.35%, while Bio-LNG and Bio-CNG saw CI increases. Overall, petroleum displacement by low-carbon fuels dropped 12.36% QoQ.
author: Aakanksha
